records.ety: [F. loigner, OF. esloignier; pref. es- (L. ex) + OF. & F. loin far, far off, L. longe, fr. longus long. See Elongate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Eloigned (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Eloigning.]

defs: To remove afar off; to withdraw.
quotes: From worldly cares he did himself eloign.

defs: To convey to a distance, or beyond the jurisdiction, or to conceal, as goods liable to distress.
quotes: The sheriff may return that the goods or beasts are eloigned.
